# chore(release): kailash 2.8.6 + dataflow 2.0.8 + kaizen 2.7.4 + nexus 2.0.2 + mcp 0.2.4

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `a90d75c65f0d` — chore(release): kailash 2.8.6 + dataflow 2.0.8 + kaizen 2.7.4 + nexus 2.0.2 + mcp 0.2.4

**Body**:

Coordinated patch release across 5 packages.

## Versions

- kailash 2.8.5 → 2.8.6
- kailash-dataflow 2.0.7 → 2.0.8
- kailash-kaizen 2.7.3 → 2.7.4
- kailash-nexus 2.0.1 → 2.0.2 (2.0.1 was never tagged/published)
- kailash-mcp 0.2.3 → 0.2.4

All framework packages updated to require kailash>=2.8.6.
Root pyproject extras updated to new framework minimums.

## Highlights

- All 63 unit test warnings resolved (PR #466) — no warnings in CI
- Production fix: datetime.utcnow() → datetime.now(UTC) in kailash-mcp
- Nexus auth guards enforced at function-wrap level for all transports (#459)
- WebSocket message handlers, middleware injection, subapp mounting (#447, #448, #449)
- A2A service migrated from raw FastAPI to Nexus (#445)
- DLQ identifier validation hoisted to __init__ (#446)
- CodeQL security alerts addressed (#444)
- Identifier length limit enforced in DataFlow dialect

## Critical fix bundled

Includes packages/kailash-nexus/src/nexus/auth/guards.py and
packages/kailash-nexus/src/nexus/errors.py — both were imported by
nexus/__init__.py in PR #459/#460 but the files themselves were
never committed. Without this commit, Nexus would fail to import
from a clean checkout. Tests for these symbols pass (15/15 in
test_nexus_bindings_459.py).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
